meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy for Freelancers: Advanced Checklist
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing to scale your freelance business. Use this advanced checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to build profitable, automated pricing models.
content.answer_block: Stop guessing your pricing. Implement value-based, tiered SaaS models by mapping feature sets to user segments. Use Antigravity to scaffold your billing infrastructure and Claude Code to integrate Stripe webhooks seamlessly. This approach turns pricing from a mystery into a predictable, automated revenue machine for your freelance clients.
content.intro:
The code is written, the UI is pixel-perfect, but the silence from your client’s bank account is deafening. You’ve built a masterpiece, yet the lack of a robust pricing strategy renders it a hobby project rather than a business. The gap between a developer and a SaaS architect is defined by the ability to monetize value, not just write syntax.

We are moving beyond flat-rate hourly billing. By leveraging advanced automation with Antigravity and the surgical precision of Claude Code, you can deploy enterprise-grade pricing engines in minutes. This isn't just about setting a price; it's about building a scalable financial engine that handles subscriptions, usage-based billing, and churn reduction automatically.

steps.title: Define Value-Based Tiers
steps.description: Don't price based on effort. Map features to specific user outcomes to create a high-converting 'Good, Better, Best' structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SaaS pricing tier structure that separates features by user intent: Hobbyist, Pro, and Enterprise. Include constraints for each tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a pricing tier configuration file based on these constraints'

steps.title: Architecting the Database Schema
steps.description: Your database needs to handle subscriptions, trial periods, and feature flags without breaking under load.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema for a SaaS subscription model using Prisma, including fields for subscription_status, stripe_customer_id, and current_period_end.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add schema.prisma 'Add subscription-related fields to the User model'

steps.title: Stripe Integration Scaffolding
steps.description: Avoid boilerplate bloat. Use automated tools to wire up your checkout sessions and customer portal redirects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Stripe checkout session handler for a Next.js API route that supports multiple pricing tiers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement Stripe checkout session logic in /api/checkout'

steps.title: Webhook Event Handling
steps.description: Sync your database state with Stripe events to ensure access is revoked or granted the millisecond a payment clears.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Stripe webhook listener that validates signatures and updates the user subscription status in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a secure webhook handler for stripe events'

steps.title: Implementing Feature Gating
steps.description: Wrap your protected features in a middleware or HOC that validates the user's active subscription status.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a TypeScript utility function to check if a user has access to a specific feature based on their current plan.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a feature-gating middleware for the application routes'

pro_tips: Always offer an annual plan with 2 months free to boost upfront cash flow and reduce churn.
Use usage-based billing caps to prevent server abuse from 'power users' on lower tiers.
Never hardcode prices; use environment variables or a remote config service for instant pricing adjustments.
Implement a 'grace period' logic in your webhook handler to prevent immediate service disruption on failed payments.
Always include a 'Contact Sales' CTA on your Enterprise tier to capture high-ticket leads that don't fit standard pricing.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing engine is live, A/B test your landing page headlines against your pricing tiers to see which value proposition converts the highest.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for free and start engineering for profit. Use these tools to turn your next freelance delivery into a recurring revenue powerhouse. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
